what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG stop icon as a rounded square outline using a single rect element.
observations: The icon has no fill and uses currentColor for its stroke.
The stroke width is 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
The visible shape is a 14 by 14 rectangle positioned at x="5" and y="5" with rx="1".
standouts: Single rect element supplies the full icon geometry.
The stroke inherits color from the surrounding context.
conventions: Uses common inline SVG icon attributes: viewBox, fill="none", stroke="currentColor", stroke-width, stroke-linecap, and stroke-linejoin.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are included in the SVG itself.
notes: A compact 24px stop icon drawn as a small rounded square outline.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consuming context?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a stop icon, and the file renders a square stop-control glyph.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable stop-action icon that adapts to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Provides a themeable outline SVG asset representing a stop action or state with a simple rounded-square glyph.
summary_for_embedding: This is a 24x24 currentColor outline SVG stop icon made from one rounded rectangle.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing a stop action or state as a reusable design-system icon.
